findings.threshold: p99 <= 50ms command submission (REST -> 202 Accepted, normal load)
evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.IntegrationTests/ContractTests/CommandLifecycleTests.cs - E2E contract tests, no p99 percentile calculation
findings.description: No controller-level bounded-timing assertion for command submission and no sustained-load harness. Telemetry wiring (EventStoreActivitySource.ProcessCommand, Submit) is in place for in-prod measurement, but pre-release latency under the stated 100 cmd/sec envelope is uncharacterized.
recommendations: Add a dedicated k6/NBomber scenario hitting /commands at 100 cmd/sec with p99 <= 50ms assertion
Tag histogram metric eventstore.command.submit.duration and alert on p99 > 50ms in staging
Stand up a CI perf-lab job (separate workflow) that runs the scenario against the Aspire topology

findings.threshold: p99 <= 200ms E2E command lifecycle (API receipt -> pub/sub event published)
evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.IntegrationTests/ContractTests/CommandLifecycleTests.cs - validates lifecycle correctness, not latency distribution
findings.description: Lifecycle is functionally verified end-to-end, but no test measures the distribution of receipt-to-publish latencies under load. Without a load harness the 200ms p99 threshold is unvalidated.
recommendations: Extend command-path load scenario to measure end-to-end span (EventStore.Submit -> EventStore.Events.EventsPublish) via OpenTelemetry traces
Export OTLP to a lab collector and compute p99 over >= 5 minute steady-state window

findings.threshold: p99 <= 10ms event append (actor persist -> state store ack)
findings.description: EventPersister has unit coverage for sequence number integrity, concurrency conflict handling, and envelope construction, but no latency assertion. 10ms is aggressive and must be validated against the real DAPR state store under load.
recommendations: Add BenchmarkDotNet micro-benchmark for EventPersister.PersistAsync against an in-memory IActorStateManager
Add an integration perf test against real DAPR state store (Redis) measuring ack latency under 100 cmd/sec

findings.threshold: p99 <= 50ms actor cold activation (snapshot + tail events)
evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/ActorStateMachineTests.cs - functional pipeline-state checkpointing only, no cold-activation timing
findings.description: Single-sample stopwatch assertion with NSubstitute mock state manager is a micro-benchmark on mock I/O, not an NFR validation. Real cold activation involves DAPR state store reads for metadata + snapshot + tail events, plus actor runtime overhead. ActorStateMachineTests cover state machine correctness but do not time cold activation.
recommendations: Replace mock-based timing with a BenchmarkDotNet benchmark against InMemoryStateManager at minimum
Add an integration test measuring the full AggregateActor OnActivateAsync against real DAPR, with p99 computed over >= 100 samples

findings.threshold: p99 <= 50ms pub/sub delivery (persistence -> subscriber delivery ack)
findings.description: Pub/sub delivery is dominated by DAPR sidecar + broker (Redis/Kafka) which is untested for p99 latency in this repo. Telemetry activity EventStoreActivitySource.EventsPublish exists, enabling in-prod measurement.
recommendations: Add integration perf scenario that publishes 1000+ events and measures persistence-timestamp-to-subscriber-receipt delta
Instrument subscriber receipt timestamp to compute one-way latency via trace correlation

findings.threshold: Full state reconstruction <= 100ms from 1,000 events (no snapshot)
findings.description: Traceability report rates this FULL but the actual evidence is a single-sample stopwatch in a mock-backed unit test, not a statistically sound benchmark. No StateRehydrationBenchmarkTests file exists in the repo (grep-confirmed). Downgraded from FULL to CONCERN per nfr-criteria (bounded-timing-in-functional-test maps to CONCERN when target is p99/quantile-based).
recommendations: Create a BenchmarkDotNet project (e.g., perf/Hexalith.EventStore.Benchmarks) with a Rehydrate1000Events benchmark producing p50/p95/p99 statistics
Run benchmark in CI on main-branch pushes and track regression against baseline

findings.threshold: >= 100 cmd/sec per instance without latency breach
evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/BackpressureTests.cs - validates MaxPendingCommandsPerAggregate=100 backpressure behavior, not sustained throughput
findings.description: Backpressure logic is unit-tested but the actual sustained 100 cmd/sec throughput (the core scalability envelope) has never been measured. No k6/NBomber/Artillery script in the repo. This is the primary GA blocker for the command path.
recommendations: Build an NBomber or k6 scenario that drives 100-200 cmd/sec across multiple aggregates for 10 minutes
Assert p99 latency does not breach NFR1/NFR2 bounds during sustained load
Measure CPU/memory saturation to establish instance right-sizing guidance

findings.threshold: p99 <= 2ms DAPR sidecar overhead per building block call
evidence: No in-repo test can isolate DAPR sidecar overhead from application code
findings.description: DAPR infra concern - overhead is a property of the DAPR runtime itself and must be measured via DAPR metrics (dapr_http_server_latency or dapr_grpc_io_server_latency) in a running topology. Not testable from application test suite.
recommendations: Scrape DAPR sidecar Prometheus metrics in staging and configure alert for p99 > 2ms per building block
Document this NFR as infra-owned in the NFR register

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Controllers/QueriesControllerTests.cs:683-692 - 200-sample p99 calculation with 25-iteration warm-up, asserts p99 < 5ms
findings.description: Best single piece of perf evidence in the repo - proper warm-up, 200-sample p99 percentile calculation. However, runs against ConstantETagService (no DAPR round-trip) and is single-threaded sequential invocation, so it validates only the controller+mediator pipeline overhead, not the real warm-actor ETag path under concurrent load.
recommendations: Extend scenario to include real ETagService with DAPR state call for the 'warm actor' path
Add a concurrent variant (multiple threads) to catch contention issues

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/CachingProjectionActorTests.cs - functional cache-hit/cache-miss logic only, no latency assertion
findings.description: Cache semantics are validated but not cache-hit latency distribution. 10ms is tight and depends heavily on serializer + actor reentrancy cost.
recommendations: Add BenchmarkDotNet benchmark for cache-hit path with realistic projection payload sizes (1KB, 10KB, 100KB)
Assert p99 < 10ms for each payload bucket

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/CachingProjectionActorTests.cs - functional rebuild path only, no timing
findings.description: Cache miss triggers projection rebuild from event stream - cost scales with stream length. No test measures rebuild latency as a function of event count.
recommendations: Parameterized benchmark: projection rebuild for streams of 10/100/1000/10000 events
Assert p99 <= 200ms for the target stream size (likely 100-500 events per snapshot boundary)

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/SignalR/SignalRProjectionChangedBroadcasterTests.cs:75 - 50-sample p99 calculation with warm-up, asserts p99 < 100ms
findings.description: Proper p99 assertion but against a mocked IHubContext (NSubstitute) - measures only the broadcaster's dispatch overhead, not end-to-end publish-to-client delivery. Real SignalR transport (WebSocket) latency is untested.
recommendations: Add integration test with real Kestrel + SignalR hub and WebSocket client, measuring server-emit to client-receive delta
Scale to 100+ concurrent subscribers to validate hub fanout cost

evidence: No concurrent query load test exists in the repo
findings.description: Zero evidence for the query-path throughput envelope. This is the #1 query-path GA risk. Per nfr-criteria, a defined target with no evidence = FAIL (not CONCERN).
recommendations: Build k6/NBomber scenario driving 1000 concurrent GET query requests/sec across mixed cache-hit and cache-miss ratios
Measure sustained throughput and CPU/memory at steady state
Required gate for GA - must demonstrate the envelope before production cutover

findings.description: Admin API controllers and services have functional coverage but no timing assertions. Targets are loose (500ms read, 2s write) and likely met in practice, but unvalidated.
recommendations: Add lightweight load scenario (10 rps) targeting each Admin API endpoint with p99 assertion
Instrument admin spans via ActivitySource and alert on SLA breach in staging

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.UI.Tests/Pages/*.cs - bunit render assertions, no wall-clock timing
findings.description: UI tests validate that components render correctly, but initial-paint/TTI measurement is absent. No Lighthouse or Core Web Vitals run in CI. SignalR update latency is partially covered by NFR38's broadcaster test but not end-to-end to the browser.
recommendations: Add Lighthouse CI job targeting /admin with budgets (LCP <= 2s, TTI <= 2s)
Extend Playwright smoke to measure time from SignalR message arrival to DOM update

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.Cli.Tests/VersionFlagTests.cs:32 - WaitForExit(5000) confirms process exits within 5s (loose upper bound)
findings.description: Traceability report rates this FULL, but the evidence is only a 5-second process-exit timeout (not a tight p99 <= 3s measurement). The 5s timeout is a hang-detector, not an NFR assertion. Downgraded to CONCERN.
recommendations: Tighten WaitForExit to 3000ms and assert process.ExitCode == 0 within that budget
Add a test that runs each major subcommand (health, stream, projection, tenant, snapshot, backup, config) and asserts wall-clock <= 3s
Consider ReadyToRun/NativeAOT publish to meet 3s budget on cold disk

evidence: tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.Mcp.Tests - functional only, no timing assertions
findings.description: MCP server is functionally tested for resource enumeration and invocation but has no latency assertion. 1s p99 is loose and likely met, but unvalidated.
recommendations: Add p99 timing assertion (100-sample warm + 200-sample measure) for each MCP tool invocation
Expose MCP duration metric and alert on breach

priority_actions: 1. (GA-BLOCKER) Introduce a load-testing framework - k6 or NBomber - at perf/Hexalith.EventStore.LoadTests/ with scenarios for 100 cmd/sec (NFR7) and 1000 query/sec (NFR39)
2. (GA-BLOCKER) Run those scenarios against the Aspire topology in a dedicated CI perf-lab workflow (nightly or on-demand), emitting p99 percentiles per NFR

3. Add a BenchmarkDotNet project (perf/Hexalith.EventStore.Benchmarks/) for NFR3 (event append), NFR4 (cold activation), NFR6 (1000-event rehydration), NFR36 (cache hit), NFR37 (cache miss) with statistically sound p50/p95/p99 output
4. Tighten VersionFlagTests process timeout from 5000ms to 3000ms and add cold-start tests for each CLI subcommand to give NFR42 a real assertion
5. Add DAPR sidecar Prometheus scraping + alert rule for p99 > 2ms (NFR8) in staging
6. Add Lighthouse CI job against the Admin UI with budgets per NFR41 (LCP <= 2s, TTI <= 2s)
7. Add end-to-end SignalR transport latency test (server-emit -> browser DOM update) for NFR38/NFR41
8. Add Admin API and MCP p99 timing tests (NFR40, NFR43) alongside existing functional tests
9. Document ownership: NFR8 is infra-owned (DAPR runtime), NFR1-NFR7, NFR35-NFR43 are app-owned and must ship with pre-release evidence
summary: Performance NFR domain is HIGH risk: there is no load-testing framework in the repository (grep-confirmed no k6, NBomber, BenchmarkDotNet, Artillery, or LoadTest files), and the GitHub Actions CI/release workflows contain no performance benchmark job - this is the #1 pre-GA risk. All existing 'performance' evidence consists of bounded-timing assertions inside functional unit/integration tests (mostly single-sample Stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds assertions against NSubstitute mocks), with only two tests performing proper p99 percentile calculations (QueriesControllerTests:683-692 for NFR35 and SignalRProjectionChangedBroadcasterTests:75 for NFR38) - and even those run against mocked dependencies. Of 17 NFRs: 1 NOT-TESTABLE (NFR8 - infra), 1 FAIL (NFR39 - no query load evidence), 15 CONCERN (including NFR6 and NFR42 which the traceability report rates FULL but are actually bounded-timing-only); OpenTelemetry wiring via EventStoreActivitySource is in place, making in-prod p99 measurement feasible once a perf-lab and staging alerting pipeline are stood up, but production cutover without validating the 100 cmd/sec and 1000 query/sec envelopes would be reckless.
